text: Golden Retrievers are a high-energy sporting breed and often need closer to 60 minutes of exercise a day, not just 30, to avoid boredom-driven chewing.
Beagles are a breed especially prone to overeating and obesity, so measured portions matter more for them than free-feeding kibble.
Tabby cats are food-motivated and prone to weight gain, so interactive play right before meals is especially effective for them.
Siamese cats are prone to dental disease as a breed; a dental check beyond the standard annual exam is often recommended.
Holland Lops have dense fur and folded ears that need regular checks for wax buildup, in addition to normal brushing.
